fault_hypothesis: The comparison on line 63 uses `>` instead of `<`, so a user is enrolled when their bucket value exceeds the percentage rather than when it falls below it. This inverts the enrollment logic, causing 100% rollout to miss users with bucket==1.0, 0% rollout to enroll users with bucket>0, and breaking monotonicity.

spec.expr: (percentage == 1.0 and __cbfl_result == True) or (percentage == 0.0 and __cbfl_result == False) or (percentage > 0.0 and percentage < 1.0)

constraints.intent: At 100% rollout every user must be enrolled (True) and at 0% rollout no user must be enrolled (False).
The computed bucket must be in [0,1] before being compared to percentage.
If bucket is strictly less than 1.0 then a 100% rollout (percentage=1.0) should enroll the user, which is only possible with `<` comparison not `>`.
A 100% rollout must never return False for any user.
